name: test.pdf
text: CloudSync API Documentation Internal reference documentation for the CloudSync file synchronization service. This document contains two dated revisions of the same API section, provided for extraction and contradiction-detection testing purposes. Authentication and Rate Limits Version 1.0 — Released March 2025 The CloudSync API requires an API key for authentication on all endpoints. The API key must be included in the Authorization header of every request. CloudSync issues API keys through the developer portal. The developer portal is maintained by the CloudSync Platform team. The standard rate limit for free tier accounts is 100 requests per minute. The rate limit for enterprise accounts is 1000 requests per minute. CloudSync enforces rate limits using a token bucket algorithm. The token bucket algorithm resets every 60 seconds. The maximum file size for a single upload is 10 megabytes. Files larger than 10 megabytes are rejected with a 413 status code. CloudSync stores uploaded files in Amazon S3. Amazon S3 buckets are replicated across three availability zones. The CloudSync API supports OAuth 2.0 as an alternative authentication method. OAuth 2.0 tokens expire after 24 hours. Refresh tokens are valid for 90 days. The authentication service is built on top of the Okta platform. Authentication and Rate Limits Version 2.0 — Released June 2026 The CloudSync API requires an API key for authentication on all endpoints. The API key must be included in the Authorization header of every request. CloudSync issues API keys through the developer portal. The developer portal is now maintained by the Identity and Access team. The standard rate limit for free tier accounts is 50 requests per minute. The rate limit for enterprise accounts is 2000 requests per minute. CloudSync enforces rate limits using a sliding window algorithm. The sliding window algorithm recalculates every 10 seconds. The maximum file size for a single upload is 25 megabytes. Files larger than 25 megabytes are rejected with a 413 status code. CloudSync stores uploaded files in Google Cloud Storage. Google Cloud Storage buckets are replicated across two regions. The CloudSync API no longer supports OAuth 2.0 as an authentication method. OAuth 2.0 support was deprecated in version 2.0. CloudSync now requires API keys for all integrations. The authentication service is built on top of the Auth0 platform.
